    Hey Folks, 

    \n

    We\'re trying new (read: bigger) venues on for size in 2015, starting with the wonderful new ExChange space in the BrightLane building for our February meet-up. 

    \n

    This month we have our own Jorge Villalobos waxing on building faux-dynamic, SEO-friendly sites with Ember + Middleman, Precision Nutrition\'s Justin Giancola will be giving a lightning talk on using redis as a proxy when developing ember applications, and Taras Mankovski will be sharing some tips and tricks regarding Ember Table. 

    \n

    Also, special thanks to Brightlane (an awesome new co-working facility) for donating their gorgeous new space to us for the evening as well! Check them out at http://brightlane.ca  

    \n

    See you soon! And as another aside, sign up for the mailing list at http://torontoemberjs.com/to start getting the 411 of our meet-ups directly, including more in-depth insights into our speaker content and more info. on Toronto Ember happenings in 2015.

   377	  def calculate_dominant_color!(local_path = nil)
   378	    color = nil
   379	
   380	    color = "" if !FileHelper.is_supported_image?("image.#{extension}") || extension == "svg"
   381	
   382	    if color.nil?
   383	      local_path ||=
   384	        if local?
   385	          Discourse.store.path_for(self)
   386	        else
   387	          Discourse.store.download(self)
   388	        end
   389	
   390	      if local_path.nil?
   391	        # Download failed. Could be too large to download, or file could be missing in s3
   392	        color = ""
   393	      end
   394	
   395	      color ||=
   396	        begin
   397	          data =
   398	            Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
   399	              "nice",
   400	              "-n",
   401	              "10",
   402	              "convert",
   403	              local_path,
   404	              "-depth",
   405	              "8",
   406	              "-resize",
   407	              "1x1",
   408	              "-define",
   409	              "histogram:unique-colors=true",
   410	              "-format",
   411	              "%c",
   412	              "histogram:info:",
   413	              timeout: DOMINANT_COLOR_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
   414	            )
   415	
   416	          # Output format:
   417	          # 1: (110.873,116.226,93.8821) #6F745E srgb(43.4798%,45.5789%,36.8165%)
   418	
   419	          color = data[/#([0-9A-F]{6})/, 1]
   420	
   421	          raise "Calculated dominant color but unable to parse output:\n#{data}" if color.nil?
   422	
   423	          color
   424	        rescue Discourse::Utils::CommandError
   425	          # Timeout or unable to parse image
   426	          # This can happen due to bad user input - ignore and save
   427	          # an empty string to prevent re-evaluation
   428	          ""
   429	        end
   430	    end
   431	
   432	    if persisted?
   433	      self.update_column(:dominant_color, color)
   434	    else
   435	      self.dominant_color = color
   436	    end
   437	  end

     3	module HasUrl

     9	    end
    10	
    11	    def extract_sha1(path)
    12	      data = extract_url(path)
    13	      return if data.blank?
    14	
    15	      sha1 = data[2]
    16	      return if sha1&.length != Upload::SHA1_LENGTH
    17	
    18	      sha1
    19	    end
    20	
    21	    def get_from_url(url)
    22	      return if url.blank?
    23	
    24	      uri =
    25	        begin
    26	          URI(UrlHelper.unencode(url))
    27	        rescue URI::Error
    28	        end
    29	
    30	      return if uri&.path.blank?
    31	      data = extract_url(uri.path)
    32	
    33	      if data.blank?
    34	        result = nil
    35	        result ||= self.find_by(url: uri.path)
    36	        return result
    37	      end
    38	
    39	      result = nil
    40	
    41	      if self.name == "Upload"
    42	        sha1 = data[2]
    43	        result = self.find_by(sha1: sha1) if sha1&.length == Upload::SHA1_LENGTH
    44	      end
    45	
    46	      result || self.find_by("url LIKE ?", "%#{data[1]}")
    47	    end
    48	
    49	    def get_from_urls(upload_urls)
    50	      urls = []
    51	      sha1s = []
    52	
    53	      upload_urls.each do |url|
    54	        next if url.blank?
    55	
    56	        uri =
    57	          begin
    58	            URI(UrlHelper.unencode(url))
    59	          rescue URI::Error
    60	          end
    61	
    62	        next if uri&.path.blank?
    63	        urls << uri.path
    64	
    65	        if data = extract_url(uri.path).presence
    66	          urls << data[1]
    67	          sha1s << data[2] if self.name == "Upload"
    68	        end
    69	      end
    70	
    71	      self.where(url: urls).or(self.where(sha1: sha1s))
    72	    end
    73	  end
    74	end

     3	class OptimizedImage < ActiveRecord::Base
     4	  include HasUrl
     5	  belongs_to :upload
     6	
     7	  # BUMP UP if optimized image algorithm changes
     8	  VERSION = 2
     9	  URL_REGEX = %r{(/optimized/\dX[/\.\w]*/([a-zA-Z0-9]+)[\.\w]*)}
    10	
    11	  def self.lock(upload_id, width, height)
    12	    @hostname ||= Discourse.os_hostname
    13	    # note, the extra lock here ensures we only optimize one image per machine on webs
    14	    # this can very easily lead to runaway CPU so slowing it down is beneficial and it is hijacked
    15	    #
    16	    # we can not afford this blocking in Sidekiq cause it can lead to starvation
    17	    if lock_per_machine?
    18	      DistributedMutex.synchronize("optimized_image_host_#{@hostname}") do
    19	        DistributedMutex.synchronize("optimized_image_#{upload_id}_#{width}_#{height}") { yield }
    20	      end
    21	    else
    22	      DistributedMutex.synchronize("optimized_image_#{upload_id}_#{width}_#{height}") { yield }
    23	    end
    24	  end
    25	
    26	  def self.lock_per_machine?
    27	    return @lock_per_machine if defined?(@lock_per_machine)
    28	    @lock_per_machine = !Sidekiq.server?
    29	  end
    30	
    31	  def self.lock_per_machine=(value)
    32	    @lock_per_machine = value
    33	  end
    34	
    35	  def self.create_for(upload, width, height, opts = {})
    36	    return if width <= 0 || height <= 0
    37	    return if upload.try(:sha1).blank?
    38	
    39	    # no extension so try to guess it
    40	    upload.fix_image_extension if (!upload.extension)
    41	
    42	    if !upload.extension.match?(IM_DECODERS)
    43	      if opts[:raise_on_error]
    44	        raise InvalidAccess
    45	      else
    46	        # nothing to do ... bad extension, not an image
    47	        return
    48	      end
    49	    end
    50	
    51	    # prefer to look up the thumbnail without grabbing any locks
    52	    extension = ".#{opts[:format] || upload.extension}"
    53	    thumbnail = find_by(upload_id: upload.id, width: width, height: height, extension: extension)
    54	
    55	    # correct bad thumbnail if needed
    56	    if thumbnail && (thumbnail.url.blank? || thumbnail.version != VERSION)
    57	      thumbnail.destroy!
    58	      thumbnail = nil
    59	    end
    60	
    61	    return thumbnail if thumbnail
    62	
    63	    store = Discourse.store
    64	
    65	    # create the thumbnail otherwise
    66	    original_path = store.path_for(upload)
    67	
    68	    if original_path.blank?
    69	      # download is protected with a DistributedMutex
    70	      original_path = store.download(upload)
    71	    end
    72	
    73	    if extension == ".svg" && upload.extension != "svg"
    74	      if opts[:raise_on_error]
    75	        raise InvalidAccess
    76	      else
    77	        # we can not convert any images to svg, unsupported
    78	        return
    79	      end
    80	    end
    81	
    82	    lock(upload.id, width, height) do
    83	      # may have been generated since we got the lock
    84	      thumbnail = find_by(upload_id: upload.id, width: width, height: height, extension: extension)
    85	
    86	      # return the previous thumbnail if any
    87	      return thumbnail if thumbnail
    88	
    89	      if original_path.blank?
    90	        Rails.logger.error("Could not find file in the store located at url: #{upload.url}")
    91	      else
    92	        # create a temp file with the same extension as the original
    93	
    94	        return nil if extension.length == 1
    95	
    96	        temp_file = Tempfile.new(["discourse-thumbnail", extension])
    97	        temp_path = temp_file.path
    98	
    99	        target_quality =
   100	          upload.target_image_quality(
   101	            original_path,
   102	            SiteSetting.ImageQuality.image_preview_jpg_quality,
   103	          )
   104	        opts = opts.merge(quality: target_quality) if target_quality
   105	        opts = opts.merge(upload_id: upload.id)
   106	
   107	        # special case, when "resizing" vectors we simply copy
   108	        if extension == ".svg"
   109	          FileUtils.cp(original_path, temp_path)
   110	          resized = true
   111	        elsif opts[:crop]
   112	          resized = crop(original_path, temp_path, width, height, opts)
   113	        else
   114	          resized = resize(original_path, temp_path, width, height, opts)
   115	        end
   116	
   117	        if resized
   118	          # TODO: crop vs resize should be stored in the db, quality should be stored
   119	          thumbnail =
   120	            OptimizedImage.create!(
   121	              upload_id: upload.id,
   122	              sha1: Upload.generate_digest(temp_path),
   123	              extension: extension,
   124	              width: width,
   125	              height: height,
   126	              url: "",
   127	              filesize: File.size(temp_path),
   128	              version: VERSION,
   129	            )
   130	
   131	          # store the optimized image and update its url
   132	          File.open(temp_path) do |file|
   133	            url = store.store_optimized_image(file, thumbnail, nil, secure: upload.secure?)
   134	            if url.present?
   135	              thumbnail.url = url
   136	              thumbnail.save
   137	            else
   138	              Rails.logger.error(
   139	                "Failed to store optimized image of size #{width}x#{height} from url: #{upload.url}\nTemp image path: #{temp_path}",
   140	              )
   141	            end
   142	          end
   143	        end
   144	
   145	        # close && remove temp file
   146	        temp_file.close!
   147	      end
   148	
   149	      thumbnail
   150	    end
   151	  end
   152	
   153	  def destroy
   154	    OptimizedImage.transaction do
   155	      Discourse.store.remove_optimized_image(self) if self.upload
   156	      super
   157	    end
   158	  end
   159	
   160	  def local?
   161	    !(url =~ %r{\A(https?:)?//})
   162	  end
   163	
   164	  def calculate_filesize
   165	    path =
   166	      if local?
   167	        Discourse.store.path_for(self)
   168	      else
   169	        Discourse.store.download!(self)
   170	      end
   171	    File.size(path)
   172	  end
   173	
   174	  def filesize
   175	    if size = read_attribute(:filesize)
   176	      size
   177	    else
   178	      size = calculate_filesize
   179	
   180	      self[:filesize] = size
   181	      update_columns(filesize: size) if !new_record?
   182	      size
   183	    end
   184	  end
   185	
   186	  def self.safe_path?(path)
   187	    # this matches instructions which call #to_s
   188	    path = path.to_s
   189	    return false if path != File.expand_path(path)
   190	    return false if path !~ %r{\A[\w\-\./]+\z}m
   191	    true
   192	  end
   193	
   194	  def self.ensure_safe_paths!(*paths)
   195	    paths.each { |path| raise Discourse::InvalidAccess unless safe_path?(path) }
   196	  end
   197	
   198	  IM_DECODERS = /\A(jpe?g|png|ico|gif|webp|avif|svg)\z/i
   199	
   200	  def self.prepend_decoder!(path, ext_path = nil, opts = nil)
   201	    opts ||= {}
   202	
   203	    # This logic is a little messy but the result of using mocks for most
   204	    # of the image tests. The idea here is you shouldn't trust the "original"
   205	    # path of a file to figure out its extension. However, in certain cases
   206	    # such as generating the loading upload thumbnail, we force the format,
   207	    # and this allows us to use the forced format in that case.
   208	    extension = nil
   209	    if (opts[:format] && path != ext_path)
   210	      extension = File.extname(path)[1..-1]
   211	    else
   212	      extension = File.extname(opts[:filename] || ext_path || path)[1..-1]
   213	    end
   214	
   215	    if !extension || !extension.match?(IM_DECODERS)
   216	      raise Discourse::InvalidAccess.new("Unsupported extension: #{extension}")
   217	    end
   218	    "#{extension}:#{path}"
   219	  end
   220	
   221	  def self.thumbnail_or_resize
   222	    SiteSetting.strip_image_metadata ? "thumbnail" : "resize"
   223	  end
   224	
   225	  def self.resize_instructions(from, to, dimensions, opts = {})
   226	    ensure_safe_paths!(from, to)
   227	
   228	    # note FROM my not be named correctly
   229	    from = prepend_decoder!(from, to, opts)
   230	    to = prepend_decoder!(to, to, opts)
   231	
   232	    instructions = ["convert", "#{from}[0]"]
   233	
   234	    instructions << "-colors" << opts[:colors].to_s if opts[:colors]
   235	
   236	    instructions << "-quality" << opts[:quality].to_s if opts[:quality]
   237	
   238	    # NOTE: ORDER is important!
   239	    instructions.concat(
   240	      %W[
   241	        -auto-orient
   242	        -gravity
   243	        center
   244	        -background
   245	        transparent
   246	        -#{thumbnail_or_resize}
   247	        #{dimensions}^
   248	        -extent
   249	        #{dimensions}
   250	        -interpolate
   251	        catrom
   252	        -unsharp
   253	        2x0.5+0.7+0
   254	        -interlace
   255	        none
   256	        -profile
   257	        #{File.join(Rails.root, "vendor", "data", "RT_sRGB.icm")}
   258	        #{to}
   259	      ],
   260	    )

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	require "fastimage"
     4	
     5	class UploadCreator
     6	  TYPES_TO_CROP = %w[avatar card_background custom_emoji profile_background].each(&:freeze)
     7	
     8	  ALLOWED_SVG_ELEMENTS = %w[
     9	    circle
    10	    clipPath
    11	    defs
    12	    ellipse
    13	    feGaussianBlur
    14	    filter
    15	    g
    16	    line
    17	    linearGradient
    18	    marker
    19	    path
    20	    polygon
    21	    polyline
    22	    radialGradient
    23	    rect
    24	    stop
    25	    style
    26	    svg
    27	    text
    28	    textPath
    29	    tref
    30	    tspan
    31	    use
    32	  ].each(&:freeze)
    33	
    34	  # Available options
    35	  #  - type (string)
    36	  #  - origin (string)
    37	  #  - for_group_message (boolean)
    38	  #  - for_theme (boolean)
    39	  #  - for_private_message (boolean)
    40	  #  - pasted (boolean)
    41	  #  - for_export (boolean)
    42	  #  - for_gravatar (boolean)
    43	  #  - skip_validations (boolean)
    44	  def initialize(file, filename, opts = {})
    45	    @file = file
    46	    @filename = (filename || "").gsub(/[^[:print:]]/, "")
    47	    @upload = Upload.new(original_filename: @filename, filesize: 0)
    48	    @opts = opts
    49	    @filesize = @opts[:filesize] if @opts[:external_upload_too_big]
    50	    @opts[:validate] = (
    51	      if opts[:skip_validations].present?
    52	        !ActiveRecord::Type::Boolean.new.cast(opts[:skip_validations])
    53	      else
    54	        true
    55	      end
    56	    )
    57	  end
    58	
    59	  def create_for(user_id)
    60	    if filesize <= 0
    61	      @upload.errors.add(:base, I18n.t("upload.empty"))
    62	      return @upload
    63	    end
    64	
    65	    @image_info =
    66	      begin
    67	        image = FastImage.new(@file)
    68	        image.type # eager load to rescue errors early
    69	        image
    70	      rescue StandardError
    71	        nil
    72	      end
    73	    is_image = FileHelper.is_supported_image?(@filename)
    74	    is_image ||= @image_info && FileHelper.is_supported_image?("test.#{@image_info.type}")
    75	    is_image = false if @opts[:for_theme]
    76	    is_thumbnail = SiteSetting.video_thumbnails_enabled && @opts[:type] == "thumbnail"
    77	
    78	    # If this is present then it means we are creating an upload record from
    79	    # an external_upload_stub and the file is > ExternalUploadManager::DOWNLOAD_LIMIT,
    80	    # so we have not downloaded it to a tempfile. no modifications can be made to the
    81	    # file in this case because it does not exist; we simply move it to its new location
    82	    # in S3
    83	    #
    84	    # FIXME: I've added a bunch of external_upload_too_big checks littered
    85	    # throughout the UploadCreator code. It would be better to have two separate
    86	    # classes with shared methods, rather than doing all these checks all over the
    87	    # place. Needs a refactor.
    88	    external_upload_too_big = @opts[:external_upload_too_big]
    89	    sha1_before_changes = Upload.generate_digest(@file) if @file
    90	
    91	    DistributedMutex.synchronize("upload_#{user_id}_#{@filename}") do
    92	      # We need to convert HEIFs early because FastImage does not consider them as images
    93	      if convert_heif_to_jpeg? && !external_upload_too_big
    94	        convert_heif!
    95	        is_image = FileHelper.is_supported_image?("test.#{@image_info.type}")
    96	      end
    97	
    98	      if is_image && !external_upload_too_big
    99	        extract_image_info!
   100	        return @upload if @upload.errors.present?
   101	
   102	        if @image_info.type == :svg
   103	          clean_svg!
   104	        elsif @image_info.type == :ico
   105	          convert_favicon_to_png!
   106	        elsif !Rails.env.test? || @opts[:force_optimize]
   107	          convert_to_jpeg! if convert_png_to_jpeg? || should_alter_quality?
   108	          fix_orientation! if should_fix_orientation?
   109	          crop! if should_crop?
   110	          optimize! if should_optimize?
   111	          downsize! if should_downsize?
   112	          return @upload if is_still_too_big?
   113	        end
   114	
   115	        # conversion may have switched the type
   116	        image_type = @image_info.type.to_s
   117	      end
   118	
   119	      # compute the sha of the file and generate a unique hash
   120	      # which is only used for secure uploads
   121	      sha1 = Upload.generate_digest(@file) if !external_upload_too_big
   122	      unique_hash = generate_fake_sha1_hash if SiteSetting.secure_uploads ||
   123	        external_upload_too_big || is_thumbnail
   124	
   125	      # we do not check for duplicate uploads if secure uploads is
   126	      # enabled because we use a unique access hash to differentiate
   127	      # between uploads instead of the sha1, and to get around various
   128	      # access/permission issues for uploads
   129	      # We do not check for duplicate uploads for video thumbnails because
   130	      # their filename needs to match with their corresponding video. This also
   131	      # enables rebuilding the html on a topic to regenerate a thumbnail.
   132	      if !SiteSetting.secure_uploads && !external_upload_too_big && !is_thumbnail
   133	        # do we already have that upload?
   134	        @upload = Upload.find_by(sha1: sha1)
   135	
   136	        # make sure the previous upload has not failed
   137	        if @upload && @upload.url.blank?
   138	          @upload.destroy
   139	          @upload = nil
   140	        end
   141	
   142	        # return the previous upload if any
   143	        if @upload
   144	          add_metadata!
   145	          UserUpload.find_or_create_by!(user_id: user_id, upload_id: @upload.id) if user_id
   146	          return @upload
   147	        end
   148	      end
   149	
   150	      fixed_original_filename = nil
   151	
   152	      if is_image && !external_upload_too_big
   153	        current_extension = File.extname(@filename).downcase.sub("jpeg", "jpg")
   154	        expected_extension = ".#{image_type}".downcase.sub("jpeg", "jpg")
   155	
   156	        # we have to correct original filename here, no choice
   157	        # otherwise validation will fail and we can not save
   158	        # TODO decide if we only run the validation on the extension
   159	        if current_extension != expected_extension
   160	          basename = File.basename(@filename, current_extension).presence || "image"
   161	          fixed_original_filename = "#{basename}#{expected_extension}"
   162	        end
   163	      end
   164	
   165	      # create the upload otherwise
   166	      @upload = Upload.new
   167	      @upload.user_id = user_id
   168	      @upload.original_filename = fixed_original_filename || @filename
   169	      @upload.filesize = filesize
   170	      @upload.sha1 =
   171	        (
   172	          if (SiteSetting.secure_uploads? || external_upload_too_big || is_thumbnail)
   173	            unique_hash
   174	          else
   175	            sha1
   176	          end
   177	        )
   178	      @upload.original_sha1 = SiteSetting.secure_uploads? ? sha1 : nil
   179	      @upload.url = ""
   180	      @upload.origin = @opts[:origin][0...2000] if @opts[:origin]
   181	      @upload.extension = image_type || File.extname(@filename)[1..10]
   182	
   183	      if is_image && !external_upload_too_big
   184	        if @image_info.type.to_s == "svg"
   185	          w, h = [0, 0]
   186	
   187	          # identify can behave differently depending on how it's compiled and
   188	          # what programs (e.g. inkscape) are installed on your system.
   189	          # 'MSVG:' forces ImageMagick to use internal routines and behave
   190	          # consistently whether it's running from our docker container or not
   191	          begin
   192	            w, h =
   193	              Discourse::Utils
   194	                .execute_command(
   195	                  "identify",
   196	                  "-ping",
   197	                  "-format",
   198	                  "%w %h",
   199	                  "MSVG:#{@file.path}",
   200	                  timeout: Upload::MAX_IDENTIFY_SECONDS,
   201	                )
   202	                .split(" ")
   203	                .map(&:to_i)
   204	          rescue StandardError
   205	            # use default 0, 0
   206	          end
   207	        else
   208	          w, h = @image_info.size
   209	        end
   210	
   211	        @upload.thumbnail_width, @upload.thumbnail_height = ImageSizer.resize(w, h)
   212	        @upload.width, @upload.height = w, h
   213	        @upload.animated = animated?
   214	        @upload.calculate_dominant_color!(@file.path)

   432	    command << to

   435	      *command,

   437	      timeout: MAX_CONVERT_FORMAT_SECONDS,
   438	    )
   439	  end

I’ve got the core contract now: `Upload` owns storage removal, short URL/sha resolution, secure status, thumbnails, optimized video links, and reference cleanup. Next I’m enumerating every model/service/job/controller/plugin that either stores `upload_id`, resolves upload URLs, or fans out work from an upload.

Accomplished endurance athlete, author and podcaster, Rich Roll has been a voice we have become familiar with over the years. In DEAR RICH, we hear the story behind the voice. Rich shares a letter to himself, giving an intimate view into the struggles he's faced with addiction, how running has helped bring him back to the surface and something that he is still learning: that we must overcome our fear of change to find what we are truly capable of.

I’m opening representative files for each surface now: post cooking/reference extraction, controllers, stores, jobs, backup/restore, site settings/theme assets, user/avatar/profile, plugins, and specs.

    59	    url = params[:url]

    94	  def lookup_urls
    95	    params.permit(short_urls: [])
    96	    uploads = []
    97	
    98	    if (params[:short_urls] && params[:short_urls].length > 0)
    99	      PrettyText::Helpers
   100	        .lookup_upload_urls(params[:short_urls])
   101	        .each do |short_url, paths|
   102	          uploads << { short_url: short_url, url: paths[:url], short_path: paths[:short_path] }
   103	        end

   105	
   106	    render json: uploads.to_json

   109	  def show
   110	    # do not serve uploads requested via XHR to prevent XSS
   111	    return xhr_not_allowed if request.xhr?
   112	
   113	    return render_404 if !RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.has_db?(params[:site])
   114	
   115	    begin
   116	      request_site_current_user =
   117	        request.env.delete(Auth::DefaultCurrentUserProvider::CURRENT_USER_KEY)
   118	
   119	      RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.with_connection(params[:site]) do |db|
   120	        begin
   121	          # current_user here refers to the user for the site that we are operating on
   122	          # using with_connection. If DB for the target site matches the current site
   123	          # for the request, then current_user will be the same as the request_site_current_user
   124	          return render_404 if SiteSetting.prevent_anons_from_downloading_files && current_user.nil?
   125	
   126	          upload =
   127	            Upload.find_by(sha1: params[:sha]) ||
   128	              Upload.find_by(id: params[:id], url: request.env["PATH_INFO"])
   129	
   130	          if upload.present?
   131	            if !Discourse.store.internal?
   132	              local_store = FileStore::LocalStore.new
   133	              return render_404 unless local_store.has_been_uploaded?(upload.url)
   134	            end
   135	
   136	            send_file_local_upload(upload)
   137	          else
   138	            render_404
   139	          end
   140	        end
   141	      end
   142	    ensure
   143	      request.env[Auth::DefaultCurrentUserProvider::CURRENT_USER_KEY] = request_site_current_user
   144	    end
   145	  end
   146	
   147	  def show_short
   148	    # do not serve uploads requested via XHR to prevent XSS
   149	    return xhr_not_allowed if request.xhr?
   150	
   151	    return render_404 if SiteSetting.prevent_anons_from_downloading_files && current_user.nil?
   152	
   153	    sha1 = Upload.sha1_from_base62_encoded(params[:base62])
   154	
   155	    if upload = Upload.find_by(sha1: sha1)
   156	      return handle_secure_upload_request(upload) if upload.secure? && SiteSetting.secure_uploads?
   157	
   158	      if Discourse.store.internal?
   159	        send_file_local_upload(upload)
   160	      else
   161	        redirect_to Discourse.store.url_for(upload, force_download: force_download?),
   162	                    allow_other_host: true
   163	      end
   164	    else
   165	      render_404
   166	    end
   167	  end

   169	  # Kept to avoid rebaking old posts with /show-secure-uploads/ in their

   176	  def show_secure
   177	    # do not serve uploads requested via XHR to prevent XSS
   178	    return xhr_not_allowed if request.xhr?
   179	
   180	    path_with_ext =
   181	      params[:extension].nil? ? params[:path] : "#{params[:path]}.#{params[:extension]}"
   182	    upload = upload_from_path_and_extension(path_with_ext)
   183	
   184	    return render_404 if upload.blank?
   185	
   186	    return render_404 if SiteSetting.prevent_anons_from_downloading_files && current_user.nil?
   187	    return handle_secure_upload_request(upload, path_with_ext) if SiteSetting.secure_uploads?
   188	
   189	    # we don't want to 404 here if secure uploads gets disabled
   190	    # because all posts with secure uploads will show broken media
   191	    # until rebaked, which could take some time
   192	    #
   193	    # if the upload is still secure, that means the ACL is probably still
   194	    # private, so we don't want to go to the CDN url just yet otherwise we
   195	    # will get a 403. if the upload is not secure we assume the ACL is public
   196	    signed_secure_url =
   197	      Discourse.store.signed_url_for_path(
   198	        path_with_ext,
   199	        filename: upload.original_filename,
   200	        include_content_disposition: true,
   201	      )
   202	    redirect_to upload.secure? ? signed_secure_url : Discourse.store.cdn_url(upload.url),
   203	                allow_other_host: true
   204	  end

   206	  def handle_secure_upload_request(upload, path_with_ext = nil)
   207	    check_secure_upload_permission(upload)
   208	
   209	    # defaults to public: false, so only cached by the client browser
   210	    cache_seconds =
   211	      SiteSetting.s3_presigned_get_url_expires_after_seconds - SECURE_REDIRECT_GRACE_SECONDS
   212	    expires_in cache_seconds.seconds
   213	
   214	    # url_for figures out the full URL, handling multisite DBs,
   215	    # and will return a presigned URL for the upload
   216	    if path_with_ext.blank?
   217	      return(
   218	        redirect_to Discourse.store.url_for(upload, force_download: force_download?),
   219	                    allow_other_host: true
   220	      )
   221	    end
   222	
   223	    redirect_to Discourse.store.signed_url_for_path(
   224	                  path_with_ext,
   225	                  expires_in: SiteSetting.s3_presigned_get_url_expires_after_seconds,
   226	                  force_download: force_download?,
   227	                  filename: upload.original_filename,
   228	                  include_content_disposition: true,
   229	                ),
   230	                allow_other_host: true
   231	  end

    42	    # Only include if association is already loaded to avoid N+1 queries

    44	    object.association(:optimized_videos).loaded? && object.optimized_videos.first.present?

     6	  end

     8	  def to_markdown(display_name: nil)
     9	    if FileHelper.is_supported_image?(@upload.original_filename)
    10	      image_markdown(display_name: display_name)
    11	    elsif FileHelper.is_supported_playable_media?(@upload.original_filename)
    12	      playable_media_markdown(display_name: display_name)
    13	    else
    14	      attachment_markdown(display_name: display_name)

    19	    display_name ||= @upload.original_filename
    20	    "![#{self.class.escape_markdown(display_name)}|#{@upload.width}x#{@upload.height}](#{@upload.short_url})"
    21	  end
    22	
    23	  def attachment_markdown(display_name: nil, with_filesize: true)
    24	    human_filesize = with_filesize ? " (#{@upload.human_filesize})" : ""
    25	    display_name ||= @upload.original_filename
    26	
    27	    "[#{self.class.escape_markdown(display_name)}|attachment](#{@upload.short_url})#{human_filesize}"
    28	  end
    29	
    30	  def playable_media_markdown(display_name: nil)
    31	    type =

    33	        "audio"
    34	      elsif FileHelper.is_supported_video?(@upload.original_filename)
    35	        "video"
    36	      end
    37	    return attachment_markdown if !type
    38	    display_name ||= @upload.original_filename
    39	    "![#{self.class.escape_markdown(display_name)}|#{type}](#{@upload.short_url})"
    40	  end

    43	    text.gsub(/[\\*_~`\[\]|]/) { |c| "\\#{c}" }

     3	module SecureUploadEndpointHelpers
     4	  include ActiveSupport::Concern
     5	
     6	  def upload_from_path_and_extension(path_with_ext)
     7	    sha1 = File.basename(path_with_ext, File.extname(path_with_ext))
     8	    # this takes care of optimized image requests
     9	    sha1 = sha1.partition("_").first if sha1.include?("_")
    10	    Upload.find_by(sha1: sha1)
    11	  end
    12	
    13	  def upload_from_full_url(url)
    14	    Upload.find_by(sha1: Upload.sha1_from_long_url(url))
    15	  end
    16	
    17	  def check_secure_upload_permission(upload)
    18	    if upload.access_control_post_id.present?
    19	      raise Discourse::InvalidAccess if current_user.nil? && SiteSetting.login_required
    20	      raise Discourse::InvalidAccess if !guardian.can_see?(upload.access_control_post)
    21	    else
    22	      raise Discourse::NotFound if current_user.nil?
    23	    end
    24	  end
    25	end

   118	    def get_path_for(type, id, sha, extension)
   119	      depth = get_depth_for(id)
   120	      tree = File.join(*sha[0, depth].chars, "")

   124	    def get_path_for_upload(upload)
   125	      # try to extract the path from the URL instead of calculating it,
   126	      # because the calculated path might differ from the actual path
   127	      if upload.url.present? && (path = upload.url[UPLOAD_PATH_REGEX, 1])
   128	        return prefix_path(path)
   129	      end
   130	
   131	      extension =
   132	        if upload.extension
   133	          ".#{upload.extension}"
   134	        else
   135	          # Maintain backward compatibility before Jobs::MigrateUploadExtensions runs
   136	          File.extname(upload.original_filename)
   137	        end
   138	
   139	      get_path_for("original", upload.id, upload.sha1, extension)
   140	    end

   142	    def get_path_for_optimized_image(optimized_image)
   143	      # try to extract the path from the URL instead of calculating it,
   144	      # because the calculated path might differ from the actual path
   145	      if optimized_image.url.present? && (path = optimized_image.url[OPTIMIZED_IMAGE_PATH_REGEX, 1])

   147	      end
   148	
   149	      upload = optimized_image.upload
   150	      version = optimized_image.version || 1
   151	      extension =
   152	        "_#{version}_#{optimized_image.width}x#{optimized_image.height}#{optimized_image.extension}"
   153	      get_path_for("optimized", upload.id, upload.sha1, extension)
   154	    end

   170	      path = get_cache_path_for(filename)
   171	      dir = File.dirname(path)
   172	      FileUtils.mkdir_p(dir) unless Dir.exist?(dir)
   173	      FileUtils.cp(file.path, path)

   184	    def perform_download(object, max_file_size_kb: nil)
   185	      DistributedMutex.synchronize("download_#{object.sha1}", validity: 3.minutes) do
   186	        name = object.respond_to?(:original_filename) ? object.original_filename : object.url
   187	        extension = File.extname(name)
   188	        filename = "#{object.sha1}#{extension}"
   189	
   190	        unless File.exist?(get_cache_path_for(filename))
   191	          max_file_size_kb ||= [
   192	            SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb,
   193	            SiteSetting.max_attachment_size_kb,
   194	          ].max.kilobytes
   195	
   196	          if object.respond_to?(:secure) ? object.secure? : object.upload.secure?
   197	            url =
   198	              Discourse.store.signed_url_for_path(object.url, include_content_disposition: false)
   199	          else
   200	            url = Discourse.store.cdn_url(object.url)
   201	          end
   202	
   203	          url = "#{SiteSetting.scheme}:#{url}" if url =~ %r{\A//}
   204	
   205	          file =
   206	            FileHelper.download(
   207	              url,
   208	              max_file_size: max_file_size_kb,

    63	      if Dir.exist?(Discourse.store.tombstone_dir)
    64	        Discourse::Utils.execute_command(

    81	      dir = Pathname.new(path).dirname
    82	      FileUtils.mkdir_p(dir) unless Dir.exist?(dir)
    83	      # move the file to the right location

    94	      absolute_url = url.start_with?("//") ? SiteSetting.scheme + ":" + url : url
    95	      absolute_url.start_with?(absolute_base_url) || absolute_url.start_with?(absolute_base_cdn_url)

   108	      list_missing(OptimizedImage) unless skip_optimized

   112	      FileUtils.mkdir_p(File.join(public_dir, upload_path))
   113	
   114	      Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
   115	        "rsync",
   116	        "-a",
   117	        "--safe-links",
   118	        "#{source_path}/",
   119	        "#{upload_path}/",
   120	        failure_message: "Failed to copy uploads.",
   121	        chdir: public_dir,
   122	      )
   123	    end

   125	    private
   126	
   127	    def list_missing(model)
   128	      count = 0
   129	      model.find_each do |upload|
   130	        # could be a remote image
   131	        next unless upload.url =~ %r{\A/[^/]}
   132	
   133	        path = "#{public_dir}#{upload.url}"
   134	        bad = true
   135	        begin
   136	          bad = false if File.size(path) != 0
   137	        rescue StandardError
   138	          # something is messed up
   139	        end
   140	        if bad
   141	          count += 1
   142	          puts path
   143	        end
   144	      end
   145	      puts "#{count} of #{model.count} #{model.name.underscore.pluralize} are missing" if count > 0
   146	    end
   147	
   148	    def prefix_path(path)
   149	      File.join("/", upload_path, path)
   150	    end
   151	  end
   152	end

    73	    # File is an actual Tempfile on disk
    74	    #
    75	    # An existing_external_upload_key is given for cases where move_existing is specified.
    76	    # This is an object already uploaded directly to S3 that we are now moving
    77	    # to its final resting place with the correct sha and key.
    78	    #
    79	    # options
    80	    #   - filename
    81	    #   - content_type
    82	    #   - cache_locally
    83	    #   - move_existing
    84	    #   - private

    86	    def store_file(file, path, opts = {})
    87	      path = path.dup
    88	
    89	      filename = opts[:filename].presence || File.basename(path)
    90	      # cache file locally when needed
    91	      cache_file(file, File.basename(path)) if opts[:cache_locally]
    92	
    93	      cache_control = "max-age=#{SiteSetting.s3_max_age}, public, immutable"
    94	      if SiteSetting.s3_stale_while_revalidate != SiteSetting.defaults[:s3_stale_while_revalidate]
    95	        cache_control =
    96	          "#{cache_control}, stale-while-revalidate=#{SiteSetting.s3_stale_while_revalidate}"
    97	      end
    98	
    99	      options = {
   100	        cache_control: cache_control,
   101	        content_type:
   102	          opts[:content_type].presence || MiniMime.lookup_by_filename(filename)&.content_type,
   103	      }.merge(default_s3_options(secure: opts[:private]))
   104	
   105	      # Only serve inline for allowlisted safe file types (non-SVG images and PDFs)
   106	      # to prevent XSS via HTML/XML/SVG uploads. All other files force download.
   107	      # See https://github.com/discourse/discourse/commit/31e31ef44973dc4daaee2f010d71588ea5873b53
   108	      options[:content_disposition] = self.class.content_disposition_for(filename)
   109	
   110	      path.prepend(File.join(upload_path, "/")) if Rails.configuration.multisite
   111	
   112	      # if this fails, it will throw an exception
   113	      if opts[:move_existing] && opts[:existing_external_upload_key]
   114	        original_path = opts[:existing_external_upload_key]
   115	        options[:apply_metadata_to_destination] = true
   116	        path, etag = s3_helper.copy(original_path, path, options: options)
   117	        delete_file(original_path)
   118	      else
   119	        path, etag = s3_helper.upload(file, path, options)
   120	      end
   121	
   122	      # return the upload url and etag
   123	      [File.join(absolute_base_url, path), etag]
   124	    end

   142	    def has_been_uploaded?(url)
   143	      return false if url.blank?
   144	
   145	      begin
   146	        parsed_url = URI.parse(UrlHelper.encode(url))
   147	      rescue StandardError
   148	        # There are many exceptions possible here including Addressable::URI:: exceptions
   149	        # and URI:: exceptions, catch all may seem wide, but it makes no sense to raise ever
   150	        # on an invalid url here
   151	        return false
   152	      end
   153	
   154	      base_hostname = URI.parse(absolute_base_url).hostname
   155	      if url[base_hostname]
   156	        # if the hostnames match it means the upload is in the same
   157	        # bucket on s3. however, the bucket folder path may differ in
   158	        # some cases, and we do not want to assume the url is uploaded
   159	        # here. e.g. the path of the current site could be /prod and the
   160	        # other site could be /staging
   161	        if s3_bucket_folder_path.present?
   162	          return parsed_url.path.starts_with?("/#{s3_bucket_folder_path}")
   163	        else
   164	          return true
   165	        end
   166	      end
   167	
   168	      return false if SiteSetting.Upload.s3_cdn_url.blank?
   169	
   170	      s3_cdn_url = URI.parse(SiteSetting.Upload.s3_cdn_url || "")
   171	      cdn_hostname = s3_cdn_url.hostname
   172	
   173	      if cdn_hostname.presence && url[cdn_hostname] &&
   174	           (s3_cdn_url.path.blank? || parsed_url.path.starts_with?(s3_cdn_url.path))
   175	        return true
   176	      end
   177	      false
   178	    end

     6	class S3Inventory
     7	  attr_reader :type, :inventory_date, :s3_helper
     8	
     9	  CSV_KEY_INDEX = 1
    10	  CSV_ETAG_INDEX = 2
    11	  INVENTORY_PREFIX = "inventory"
    12	  INVENTORY_LAG = 2.days
    13	  WAIT_AFTER_RESTORE_DAYS = 2
    14	
    15	  def initialize(
    16	    type,
    17	    s3_inventory_bucket:,
    18	    preloaded_inventory_file: nil,
    19	    preloaded_inventory_date: nil,
    20	    s3_options: {}
    21	  )
    22	    @s3_helper = S3Helper.new(s3_inventory_bucket, "", s3_options)
    23	
    24	    if preloaded_inventory_file && preloaded_inventory_date
    25	      # Data preloaded, so we don't need to fetch it again
    26	      @preloaded_inventory_file = preloaded_inventory_file
    27	      @inventory_date = preloaded_inventory_date
    28	    end
    29	
    30	    if type == :upload
    31	      @type = "original"
    32	      @model = Upload
    33	      @scope = @model.by_users.without_s3_file_missing_confirmed_verification_status
    34	    elsif type == :optimized
    35	      @type = "optimized"
    36	      @scope = @model = OptimizedImage
    37	    else
    38	      raise "Invalid type: #{type}"
    39	    end
    40	  end
    41	
    42	  def backfill_etags_and_list_missing
    43	    if !@preloaded_inventory_file && files.blank?
    44	      error("Failed to list inventory from S3")
    45	      return
    46	    end
    47	
    48	    DistributedMutex.synchronize("s3_inventory_list_missing_#{type}", validity: 30.minutes) do
    49	      begin
    50	        download_and_decompress_files if !@preloaded_inventory_file
    51	
    52	        multisite_prefix = Discourse.store.upload_path
    53	
    54	        ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
    55	          begin
    56	            connection.exec(
    57	              "CREATE TEMP TABLE #{tmp_table_name}(url text UNIQUE, etag text, PRIMARY KEY(etag, url))",
    58	            )
    59	
    60	            connection.copy_data("COPY #{tmp_table_name} FROM STDIN CSV") do
    61	              for_each_inventory_row do |row|
    62	                key = row[CSV_KEY_INDEX]
    63	
    64	                next if Rails.configuration.multisite && key.exclude?(multisite_prefix)
    65	                next if key.exclude?("#{type}/")
    66	
    67	                url = File.join(Discourse.store.absolute_base_url, key)
    68	                connection.put_copy_data("#{CGI.unescape(url)},#{row[CSV_ETAG_INDEX]}\n")
    69	              end
    70	            end
    71	
    72	            table_name = @model.table_name
    73	
    74	            # backfilling etags
    75	            connection.async_exec(
    76	              "UPDATE #{table_name}
    77	              SET etag = #{tmp_table_name}.etag
    78	              FROM #{tmp_table_name}
    79	              WHERE #{table_name}.etag IS NULL AND
    80	                #{table_name}.url = #{tmp_table_name}.url",
    81	            )
    82	
    83	            uploads = @scope.where("updated_at < ?", inventory_date)
    84	
    85	            missing_uploads =
    86	              uploads.joins(
    87	                "LEFT JOIN #{tmp_table_name} ON #{tmp_table_name}.etag = #{table_name}.etag",
    88	              ).where("#{tmp_table_name}.etag IS NULL")
    89	
    90	            exists_with_different_etag =
    91	              missing_uploads
    92	                .joins(
    93	                  "LEFT JOIN #{tmp_table_name} inventory2 ON inventory2.url = #{table_name}.url",
    94	                )
    95	                .where.not(inventory2: { etag: nil })
    96	                .pluck(:id)
    97	
    98	            # marking as verified/not verified
    99	            if @model == Upload
   100	              sql_params = {
   101	                inventory_date: inventory_date,
   102	                invalid_etag: Upload.verification_statuses[:invalid_etag],
   103	                s3_file_missing_confirmed: Upload.verification_statuses[:s3_file_missing_confirmed],
   104	                verified: Upload.verification_statuses[:verified],
   105	                seeded_id_threshold: @model::SEEDED_ID_THRESHOLD,
   106	              }
   107	
   108	              DB.exec(<<~SQL, sql_params)
   109	                UPDATE #{table_name}
   110	                SET verification_status = :verified
   111	                WHERE etag IS NOT NULL
   112	                  AND verification_status <> :verified
   113	                  AND verification_status <> :s3_file_missing_confirmed
   114	                  AND updated_at < :inventory_date
   115	                  AND id > :seeded_id_threshold
   116	                  AND EXISTS
   117	                    (
   118	                        SELECT 1
   119	                        FROM #{tmp_table_name}
   120	                        WHERE #{tmp_table_name}.etag = #{table_name}.etag
   121	                    )
   122	              SQL
   123	
   124	              DB.exec(<<~SQL, sql_params)
   125	                UPDATE #{table_name}
   126	                SET verification_status = :invalid_etag
   127	                WHERE verification_status <> :invalid_etag
   128	                  AND verification_status <> :s3_file_missing_confirmed
   129	                  AND updated_at < :inventory_date
   130	                  AND id > :seeded_id_threshold
   131	                  AND NOT EXISTS
   132	                    (
   133	                        SELECT 1
   134	                        FROM #{tmp_table_name}
   135	                        WHERE #{tmp_table_name}.etag = #{table_name}.etag
   136	                    )
   137	              SQL
   138	            end
   139	
   140	            if (missing_count = missing_uploads.count) > 0
   141	              missing_uploads
   142	                .select(:id, :url)
   143	                .find_each do |upload|
   144	                  if exists_with_different_etag.include?(upload.id)
   145	                    log "#{upload.url} has different etag"
   146	                  else
   147	                    log upload.url
   148	                  end
   149	                end
   150	
   151	              log "#{missing_count} of #{uploads.count} #{@scope.name.underscore.pluralize} are missing"
   152	              if exists_with_different_etag.present?
   153	                log "#{exists_with_different_etag.count} of these are caused by differing etags"
   154	                log "Null the etag column and re-run for automatic backfill"
   155	              end
   156	            end
   157	
   158	            set_missing_s3_discourse_stats(missing_count)
   159	          ensure
   160	            connection.exec("DROP TABLE #{tmp_table_name}") unless connection.nil?
   161	          end
   162	        end
   163	      ensure
   164	        cleanup!
   165	      end
   166	    end
   167	  end
   168	
   169	  def for_each_inventory_row
   170	    if @preloaded_inventory_file

content.text:
1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module Jobs
     4	  class CleanUpUploads < ::Jobs::Scheduled
     5	    every 1.hour
     6	
     7	    def execute(args)
     8	      grace_period = [SiteSetting.clean_orphan_uploads_grace_period_hours, 1].max
     9	
    10	      # Always remove invalid upload records regardless of clean_up_uploads setting.
    11	      Upload
    12	        .by_users
    13	        .where(
    14	          "retain_hours IS NULL OR created_at < current_timestamp - interval '1 hour' * retain_hours",
    15	        )
    16	        .where("created_at < ?", grace_period.hour.ago)
    17	        .where(url: "")
    18	        .find_each(&:destroy!)
    19	
    20	      return unless SiteSetting.clean_up_uploads?
    21	
    22	      # Do nothing if the last cleanup was run too recently.
    23	      last_cleanup_timestamp = last_cleanup
    24	      if last_cleanup_timestamp.present? &&
    25	           (Time.zone.now.to_i - last_cleanup_timestamp) < (grace_period / 2).hours
    26	        return
    27	      end
    28	
    29	      result = Upload.by_users
    30	      Upload.unused_callbacks&.each { |handler| result = handler.call(result) }
    31	
    32	      # 1. Exclude uploads that have retain_hours set and are still within that retention period.
    33	      # 2. Exclude uploads created in the grace period.
    34	      # 3. Exclude secure uploads that have an access_control_post_id.
    35	      # 4. Exclude uploads that are link to an upload reference.
    36	      # 5. Exclude uploads that are linked to anything but a Post via UploadReference.
    37	      result =
    38	        result
    39	          .where(
    40	            "uploads.retain_hours IS NULL OR uploads.created_at < current_timestamp - interval '1 hour' * uploads.retain_hours",
    41	          )
    42	          .where("uploads.created_at < ?", grace_period.hour.ago)
    43	          .where(
    44	            "((uploads.access_control_post_id IS NULL) OR (uploads.access_control_post_id IS NOT NULL AND NOT uploads.secure))",
    45	          )
    46	          .joins("LEFT JOIN upload_references ON upload_references.upload_id = uploads.id")
    47	          .where("upload_references.upload_id IS NULL")
    48	          .with_no_non_post_relations
    49	
    50	      result.find_each do |upload|
    51	        next if Upload.in_use_callbacks&.any? { |callback| callback.call(upload) }
    52	        upload.sha1.present? ? upload.destroy : upload.delete
    53	      end
    54	
    55	      ExternalUploadStub.cleanup!
    56	
    57	      self.last_cleanup = Time.zone.now.to_i
    58	    end
    59	
    60	    def last_cleanup=(timestamp)
    61	      Discourse.redis.setex(last_cleanup_key, 7.days.to_i, timestamp.to_s)
    62	    end
    63	
    64	    def last_cleanup
    65	      timestamp = Discourse.redis.get(last_cleanup_key)
    66	      timestamp ? timestamp.to_i : timestamp
    67	    end
    68	
    69	    def reset_last_cleanup!
    70	      Discourse.redis.del(last_cleanup_key)
    71	    end
    72	
    73	    protected
    74	
    75	    def last_cleanup_key
    76	      "LAST_UPLOAD_CLEANUP"
    77	    end
    78	  end
    79	end

     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	module Jobs
     3	  class ConvertVideo < ::Jobs::Base
     4	    sidekiq_options queue: "low", concurrency: 5
     5	    MAX_RETRIES = 5
     6	    RETRY_DELAY = 30.seconds
     7	
     8	    def execute(args)
     9	      return if args[:upload_id].blank?
    10	
    11	      upload = Upload.find_by(id: args[:upload_id])
    12	      return if upload.blank?
    13	
    14	      return if OptimizedVideo.exists?(upload_id: upload.id)
    15	
    16	      if upload.url.blank?
    17	        retry_count = args[:retry_count].to_i
    18	        if retry_count < MAX_RETRIES
    19	          Jobs.enqueue_in(RETRY_DELAY, :convert_video, args.merge(retry_count: retry_count + 1))
    20	          return
    21	        else
    22	          Rails.logger.error(
    23	            "Upload #{upload.id} URL remained blank after #{MAX_RETRIES} retries when optimizing video",
    24	          )
    25	          return
    26	        end
    27	      end
    28	
    29	      adapter = VideoConversion::AdapterFactory.get_adapter(upload)
    30	
    31	      Rails.logger.error("Video conversion failed for upload #{upload.id}") if !adapter.convert
    32	    end
    33	  end
    34	end
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	module Jobs
     3	  class CheckVideoConversionStatus < ::Jobs::Base
     4	    sidekiq_options queue: "low", concurrency: 5
     5	
     6	    def execute(args)
     7	      return if args[:upload_id].blank? || args[:job_id].blank? || args[:adapter_type].blank?
     8	
     9	      upload = Upload.find_by(id: args[:upload_id])
    10	      return if upload.blank?
    11	
    12	      adapter =
    13	        VideoConversion::AdapterFactory.get_adapter(upload, adapter_type: args[:adapter_type])
    14	
    15	      status = adapter.check_status(args[:job_id])
    16	
    17	      case status
    18	      when :complete
    19	        if adapter.handle_completion(args[:job_id], args[:new_sha1])
    20	          # Successfully completed
    21	          Rails.logger.info(
    22	            "Completed video conversion for upload ID #{upload.id} and job ID #{args[:job_id]}",
    23	          )
    24	        else
    25	          # Handle completion failed
    26	          Rails.logger.error(
    27	            "Failed to handle video conversion completion for upload ID #{upload.id} and job ID #{args[:job_id]}",
    28	          )
    29	        end
    30	      when :error
    31	        Rails.logger.error(
    32	          "Video conversion job failed for upload ID #{upload.id} and job ID #{args[:job_id]}",
    33	        )
    34	      when :pending
    35	        # Re-enqueue the job to check again
    36	        Jobs.enqueue_in(30.seconds, :check_video_conversion_status, args)
    37	      end
    38	    end
    39	  end
    40	end

     4	  # Sometimes we need to update the access control metadata for a _lot_ of objects on S3 (such as when secure uploads
     5	  # is enabled), this is best spread out over many jobs instead of having to do the whole thing serially.
     6	  class SyncAccessControlForUploads < ::Jobs::Base
     7	    sidekiq_options queue: "low"
     8	
     9	    def execute(args)
    10	      return if !Discourse.store.external?
    11	      return if !args.key?(:upload_ids)
    12	
    13	      Upload
    14	        .includes(:optimized_images)
    15	        .where(id: args[:upload_ids])
    16	        .find_in_batches do |uploads|
    17	          uploads.each do |upload|
    18	            begin
    19	              Discourse.store.update_upload_access_control(upload, remove_existing_acl: true)
    20	            rescue => err
    21	              Discourse.warn_exception(
    22	                err,
    23	                message: "Failed to update upload access control",
    24	                env: {
    25	                  upload_id: upload.id,
    26	                  filename: upload.original_filename,
    27	                },
    28	              )
    29	            end
    30	          end
    31	        end
    32	    end
    33	  end
    34	end

     3	module Jobs
     4	  class UpdateAnimatedUploads < ::Jobs::Scheduled
     5	    every 1.hour
     6	
     7	    MAX_PROCESSED_GIF_IMAGES = 200
     8	
     9	    def execute(args)
    10	      Upload
    11	        .where("extension = 'gif' OR (extension IS NULL AND original_filename LIKE '%.gif')")
    12	        .where(animated: nil)
    13	        .limit(MAX_PROCESSED_GIF_IMAGES)
    14	        .each do |upload|
    15	          uri = Discourse.store.path_for(upload) || upload.url
    16	          upload.animated = FastImage.animated?(uri)
    17	          upload.save(validate: false)
    18	          upload.optimized_images.destroy_all if upload.animated
    19	        end
    20	
    21	      nil
    22	    end
    23	  end

     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module Jobs
     4	  class MigrateUploadScheme < ::Jobs::Scheduled
     5	    every 10.minutes
     6	    sidekiq_options retry: false
     7	
     8	    def execute(args)
     9	      return unless SiteSetting.migrate_to_new_scheme
    10	
    11	      # clean up failed uploads
    12	      Upload
    13	        .where("created_at < ?", 1.hour.ago)
    14	        .where("LENGTH(COALESCE(url, '')) = 0")
    15	        .find_each { |upload| upload.destroy! }
    16	
    17	      # migrate uploads to new scheme
    18	      problems = Upload.migrate_to_new_scheme(limit: 50)
    19	
    20	      problems.each do |hash|
    21	        upload_id = hash[:upload].id
    22	        Discourse.handle_job_exception(
    23	          hash[:ex],
    24	          error_context(args, "Migrating upload id #{upload_id}", upload_id: upload_id),
    25	        )
    26	      end
    27	
    28	      # clean up failed optimized images
    29	      OptimizedImage
    30	        .where("LENGTH(COALESCE(url, '')) = 0")
    31	        .find_each { |optimized_image| optimized_image.destroy! }
    32	
    33	      # Clean up orphan optimized images
    34	      OptimizedImage
    35	        .joins("LEFT JOIN uploads ON optimized_images.upload_id = uploads.id")
    36	        .where("uploads.id IS NULL")
    37	        .find_each { |optimized_image| optimized_image.destroy! }
    38	
    39	      # Clean up optimized images that needs to be regenerated
    40	      OptimizedImage
    41	        .joins(:upload)
    42	        .where("optimized_images.url NOT LIKE '%/optimized/_X/%'")
    43	        .where("uploads.url LIKE '%/original/_X/%'")
    44	        .limit(50)
    45	        .find_each do |optimized_image|
    46	          upload = optimized_image.upload
    47	          optimized_image.destroy!
    48	          upload.rebake_posts_on_old_scheme
    49	        end
    50	    end
    51	  end
    52	end

     4	  class FixS3Etags < ::Jobs::Onceoff
     5	    def execute_onceoff(args)
     6	      [Upload, OptimizedImage].each do |model|
     7	        sql =
     8	          "UPDATE #{model.table_name} SET etag = REGEXP_REPLACE(etag, '\"', '', 'g') WHERE etag LIKE '\"%\"'"
     9	        DB.exec(sql)
    10	      end
    11	    end
    12	  end
    13	end

I’m seeing the key guard pattern repeat: jobs and controllers generally `find_by` the upload and bail if missing, URL parsing uses SHA/base62 helpers, and storage resolution funnels through `Discourse.store.path_for`, `download`, `url_for`, `cdn_url`, and `signed_url_for_path`. I’m collecting the dependent model list and plugin surfaces next.

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	##
     4	# A note on determining whether an upload should be marked as secure:
     5	#
     6	# Some of these flags checked (e.g. all of the for_X flags and the opts[:type])
     7	# are only set when _initially uploading_ via UploadCreator and are not present
     8	# when an upload already exists, these will only be checked when the @creating
     9	# option is present.
    10	#
    11	# If the upload already exists the best way to figure out whether it should be
    12	# secure alongside the site settings is the access_control_post_id, because the
    13	# original post the upload is linked to has far more bearing on its security context
    14	# post-upload. If the access_control_post_id does not exist then we just rely
    15	# on the current secure? status, otherwise there would be a lot of additional
    16	# complex queries and joins to perform.
    17	#
    18	# These queries will be performed only if the @creating option is false. So if
    19	# an upload is included in a post, and it's an upload from a different source
    20	# (e.g. a category logo, site setting upload) then we will determine secure
    21	# state _based on the first place the upload was referenced_.
    22	#
    23	# NOTE: When updating this to add more cases where uploads will be marked
    24	# secure, consider uploads:secure_upload_analyse_and_update as well, which
    25	# does not use this class directly but uses an SQL version of its rules for
    26	# efficient updating of many uploads in bulk.
    27	class UploadSecurity
    28	  @@custom_public_types = []
    29	
    30	  PUBLIC_TYPES = %w[
    31	    avatar
    32	    custom_emoji
    33	    profile_background
    34	    card_background
    35	    category_logo
    36	    category_logo_dark
    37	    category_background
    38	    group_flair
    39	    badge_image
    40	    site_setting
    41	  ]
    42	
    43	  PUBLIC_UPLOAD_REFERENCE_TYPES = %w[
    44	    Badge
    45	    Category
    46	    CustomEmoji
    47	    Group
    48	    SiteSetting
    49	    ThemeField
    50	    User
    51	    UserAvatar
    52	    UserProfile
    53	  ]
    54	
    55	  def self.register_custom_public_type(type)
    56	    @@custom_public_types << type if !@@custom_public_types.include?(type)
    57	  end
    58	
    59	  # used in tests
    60	  def self.reset_custom_public_types
    61	    @@custom_public_types = []
    62	  end
    63	
    64	  def initialize(upload, opts = {})
    65	    @upload = upload
    66	    @opts = opts
    67	    @upload_type = @opts[:type]
    68	    @creating = @opts[:creating]
    69	  end
    70	
    71	  def should_be_secure?
    72	    should_be_secure_with_reason.first
    73	  end
    74	
    75	  def should_be_secure_with_reason
    76	    insecure_context_checks.each { |check, reason| return false, reason if perform_check(check) }
    77	    secure_context_checks.each do |check, reason|
    78	      return perform_check(check), reason if priority_check?(check)
    79	      return true, reason if perform_check(check)
    80	    end
    81	
    82	    [false, "no checks satisfied"]
    83	  end
    84	
    85	  private
    86	
    87	  def access_control_post
    88	    @access_control_post ||=
    89	      @upload.access_control_post_id.present? ? @upload.access_control_post : nil
    90	  end
    91	
    92	  def insecure_context_checks
    93	    {
    94	      secure_uploads_disabled: "secure uploads is disabled",
    95	      insecure_creation_for_modifiers: "one or more creation for_modifiers was satisfied",
    96	      public_type: "upload is public type",
    97	      regular_emoji: "upload is used for regular emoji",
    98	      publicly_referenced_first: "upload was publicly referenced when it was first created",
    99	    }
   100	  end
   101	
   102	  def secure_context_checks
   103	    {
   104	      login_required: "login is required",
   105	      access_control_post_should_secure_uploads: "access control post dictates security",
   106	      secure_creation_for_modifiers: "one or more creation for_modifiers was satisfied",
   107	      uploading_in_composer: "uploading via the composer",
   108	      already_secure: "upload is already secure",
   109	    }
   110	  end
   111	
   112	  # The access control check is important because that is the truest indicator
   113	  # of whether an upload should be secure or not, and thus should be returned
   114	  # immediately if there is an access control post.
   115	  def priority_check?(check)
   116	    check == :access_control_post_should_secure_uploads && access_control_post
   117	  end
   118	
   119	  def perform_check(check)
   120	    send("#{check}_check")
   121	  end
   122	
   123	  #### START PUBLIC CHECKS ####
   124	
   125	  def secure_uploads_disabled_check
   126	    !SiteSetting.secure_uploads?
   127	  end
   128	
   129	  def insecure_creation_for_modifiers_check
   130	    return false if !@creating
   131	    @upload.for_theme || @upload.for_site_setting || @upload.for_gravatar
   132	  end
   133	
   134	  def public_type_check
   135	    PUBLIC_TYPES.include?(@upload_type) || @@custom_public_types.include?(@upload_type)
   136	  end
   137	
   138	  def publicly_referenced_first_check
   139	    return false if @creating
   140	    first_reference =
   141	      @upload
   142	        .upload_references
   143	        .joins(<<~SQL)
   144	          LEFT JOIN posts ON upload_references.target_type = 'Post' AND upload_references.target_id = posts.id
   145	        SQL
   146	        .where("posts.deleted_at IS NULL")
   147	        .order("upload_references.created_at ASC, upload_references.id ASC")
   148	        .first
   149	    return false if first_reference.blank?
   150	    PUBLIC_UPLOAD_REFERENCE_TYPES.include?(first_reference.target_type)
   151	  end
   152	
   153	  def regular_emoji_check
   154	    return false if @upload.origin.blank?
   155	    uri = URI.parse(@upload.origin)
   156	    return true if Emoji.all.map(&:url).include?("#{uri.path}?#{uri.query}")
   157	    uri.path.include?("images/emoji")
   158	  end
   159	
   160	  #### END PUBLIC CHECKS ####
   161	
   162	  #--------------------------#
   163	
   164	  #### START PRIVATE CHECKS ####
   165	
   166	  def login_required_check
   167	    SiteSetting.login_required? && !SiteSetting.secure_uploads_pm_only?
   168	  end
   169	
   170	  # Whether the upload should remain secure or not after posting depends on its context,
   171	  # which is based on the post it is linked to via access_control_post_id.
   172	  #
   173	  # If that post should_secure_uploads? then the upload should also be secure.
   174	  #
   175	  # This may change to false if the upload was set to secure on upload e.g. in
   176	  # a post composer then it turned out that the post itself was not in a secure context.
   177	  #
   178	  # A post is with secure uploads if it is a private message or in a read restricted
   179	  # category. See `Post#should_secure_uploads?` for the full definition.
   180	  def access_control_post_should_secure_uploads_check
   181	    access_control_post&.should_secure_uploads?
   182	  end
   183	
   184	  def uploading_in_composer_check
   185	    @upload_type == "composer"
   186	  end
   187	
   188	  def secure_creation_for_modifiers_check
   189	    return false if !@creating
   190	    @upload.for_private_message || @upload.for_group_message
   191	  end
   192	
   193	  def already_secure_check
   194	    @upload.secure?
   195	  end
   196	
   197	  #### END PRIVATE CHECKS ####
   198	end
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	##
     4	# There are certain conditions with secure uploads when the security of
     5	# uploads will need to change depending on the context they reside in.
     6	#
     7	# For example on these conditions:
     8	#  * Topic category change
     9	#  * Topic switching between PM and public topic
    10	#  * Post moving between topics
    11	#
    12	# We need to go through all of the posts in that topic that
    13	# own uploads via access_control_post_id, then for those uploads determine
    14	# if they still need to be secure or not. For example an upload could be
    15	# secure if it is in a PM, and then when the topic gets converted to a public
    16	# topic the upload no longer needs to remain secure as it is no longer in
    17	# a secure context.
    18	class TopicUploadSecurityManager
    19	  def initialize(topic)
    20	    @topic = topic
    21	  end
    22	
    23	  def run
    24	    rebaked_posts = []
    25	    Rails.logger.debug("Updating upload security in topic #{@topic.id}")
    26	    posts_owning_uploads.each do |post|
    27	      Post.transaction do
    28	        Rails.logger.debug("Updating upload security in topic #{@topic.id} - post #{post.id}")
    29	        post.topic = @topic
    30	
    31	        secure_status_did_change =
    32	          post.owned_uploads_via_access_control.any? do |upload|
    33	            # We already have the post preloaded so we may as well
    34	            # attach it here to avoid another load in UploadSecurity
    35	            # (which is called via update_secure_status)
    36	            upload.access_control_post = post
    37	            upload.update_secure_status(source: "topic upload security")
    38	          end
    39	
    40	        if secure_status_did_change
    41	          post.rebake!
    42	          rebaked_posts << post
    43	        end
    44	        Rails.logger.debug(
    45	          "Security updated & rebake complete in topic #{@topic.id} - post #{post.id}",
    46	        )
    47	      end
    48	    end
    49	
    50	    return rebaked_posts if !SiteSetting.secure_uploads
    51	
    52	    # We only want to do this if secure uploads is enabled. If
    53	    # the setting is turned on after a site has been running
    54	    # already, we want to make sure that any post moves after
    55	    # this are handled and upload secure statuses and ACLs
    56	    # are updated appropriately, as well as setting the access control
    57	    # post for secure uploads missing it.
    58	    #
    59	    # Examples (all after secure uploads is enabled):
    60	    #
    61	    #  -> a public topic is moved to a private category after
    62	    #  -> a PM is converted to a public topic
    63	    #  -> a public topic is converted to a PM
    64	    #  -> a topic is moved from a private to a public category
    65	    posts_with_unowned_uploads.each do |post|
    66	      Post.transaction do
    67	        Rails.logger.debug(
    68	          "Setting upload access control posts in topic #{@topic.id} - post #{post.id}",
    69	        )
    70	        post.topic = @topic
    71	
    72	        secure_status_did_change =
    73	          post.uploads.any? do |upload|
    74	            first_post_upload_appeared_in =
    75	              upload.upload_references.where(target_type: "Post").first.target
    76	            if first_post_upload_appeared_in == post
    77	              upload.update(access_control_post: post)
    78	              upload.update_secure_status(source: "topic upload security")
    79	            else
    80	              false
    81	            end
    82	          end
    83	
    84	        if secure_status_did_change
    85	          post.rebake!
    86	          rebaked_posts << post
    87	        end
    88	        Rails.logger.debug(
    89	          "Completed changing access control posts #{secure_status_did_change ? "and rebaking" : ""} in topic #{@topic.id} - post #{post.id}",
    90	        )
    91	      end
    92	    end
    93	
    94	    Rails.logger.debug("Completed updating upload security in topic #{@topic.id}!")
    95	    rebaked_posts
    96	  end
    97	
    98	  private
    99	
   100	  def posts_owning_uploads
   101	    Post.where(topic_id: @topic.id, id: Upload.select(:access_control_post_id))
   102	  end
   103	
   104	  def posts_with_unowned_uploads
   105	    Post.where(
   106	      topic_id: @topic.id,
   107	      id:
   108	        UploadReference.where(
   109	          target_type: "Post",
   110	          upload: Upload.where(access_control_post_id: nil),
   111	        ).select(:target_id),
   112	    ).includes(:uploads)
   113	  end
   114	end

     3	# mixin for all Guardian methods dealing with user permissions
     4	module UserGuardian
     5	  def can_claim_reviewable_topic?(topic, automatic = false)
     6	    (SiteSetting.reviewable_claiming != "disabled" || automatic) && can_review_topic?(topic)
     7	  end
     8	
     9	  def can_pick_avatar?(user_avatar, upload)
    10	    return false unless self.user
    11	    return true if is_admin?
    12	    # can always pick blank avatar
    13	    return true if !upload
    14	    return true if user_avatar.contains_upload?(upload.id)
    15	    return true if upload.user_id == user_avatar.user_id || is_my_own?(upload)
    16	
    17	    UserUpload.exists?(upload_id: upload.id, user_id: user.id)
    18	  end
    19	
    20	  def can_edit_user?(user)
    21	    is_me?(user) || is_staff?
    22	  end
    23	
    24	  def can_edit_username?(user)
    25	    return false if SiteSetting.auth_overrides_username?
    26	    return true if is_staff?
    27	    return false if SiteSetting.username_change_period <= 0
    28	    return false if is_anonymous?
    29	    is_me?(user) && user.created_at > SiteSetting.username_change_period.days.ago
    30	  end
    31	
    32	  def can_edit_email?(user)
    33	    return false if SiteSetting.auth_overrides_email?
    34	    return false unless SiteSetting.email_editable?
    35	    return true if is_admin?
    36	    return false if is_anonymous?
    37	    return false if is_moderator? && !is_me?(user)
    38	
    39	    can_edit?(user)
    40	  end

     3	require "file_helper"
     4	
     5	class UploadValidator < ActiveModel::Validator
     6	  def validate(upload)
     7	    # staff can upload any file in PM
     8	    if (upload.for_private_message && SiteSetting.allow_staff_to_upload_any_file_in_pm)
     9	      return true if upload.user&.staff?
    10	    end
    11	
    12	    # check the attachment blocklist
    13	    if upload.for_group_message && SiteSetting.allow_all_attachments_for_group_messages
    14	      return upload.original_filename =~ SiteSetting.blocked_attachment_filenames_regex
    15	    end
    16	
    17	    extension = File.extname(upload.original_filename)[1..-1] || ""
    18	
    19	    return validate_site_setting_upload(upload, extension) if upload.for_site_setting
    20	
    21	    if upload.for_gravatar && FileHelper.supported_gravatar_extensions.include?(extension)
    22	      maximum_image_file_size(upload)
    23	      return true
    24	    end
    25	
    26	    return true if changing_upload_security?(upload)
    27	
    28	    if is_authorized?(upload, extension)
    29	      if FileHelper.is_supported_image?(upload.original_filename)
    30	        authorized_image_extension(upload, extension)
    31	        maximum_image_file_size(upload)
    32	      else
    33	        authorized_attachment_extension(upload, extension)
    34	        maximum_attachment_file_size(upload)
    35	      end
    36	    end
    37	  end
    38	
    39	  def validate_site_setting_upload(upload, extension)
    40	    unless upload.user&.staff?
    41	      upload.errors.add(:base, I18n.t("upload.unauthorized"))
    42	      return false
    43	    end
    44	
    45	    setting_opts = site_setting_type_hash(upload.site_setting_name)
    46	    authorized_extensions = setting_opts[:authorized_extensions]
    47	
    48	    if authorized_extensions.present?
    49	      unless extension_allowed_for_site_setting?(extension, authorized_extensions)
    50	        upload.errors.add(
    51	          :original_filename,
    52	          I18n.t("upload.unauthorized", authorized_extensions: authorized_extensions),
    53	        )
    54	        return false
    55	      end
    56	
    57	      validate_site_setting_file_size(upload, setting_opts)
    58	    else
    59	      unless FileHelper.is_supported_image?(upload.original_filename)
    60	        upload.errors.add(:original_filename, I18n.t("upload.images_only"))
    61	        return false
    62	      end
    63	
    64	      validate_site_setting_file_size(upload, setting_opts)
    65	    end
    66	
    67	    upload.errors.empty?
    68	  end
    69	
    70	  # this should only be run on existing records, and covers cases of
    71	  # upload.update_secure_status being run outside of the creation flow,
    72	  # where some cases e.g. have exemptions on the extension enforcement
    73	  def changing_upload_security?(upload)
    74	    !upload.new_record? &&
    75	      upload.changed_attributes.keys.all? do |attribute|
    76	        %w[secure security_last_changed_at security_last_changed_reason].include?(attribute)
    77	      end
    78	  end
    79	
    80	  def is_authorized?(upload, extension)
    81	    extension_authorized?(upload, extension, authorized_extensions(upload))
    82	  end
    83	
    84	  def authorized_image_extension(upload, extension)
    85	    extension_authorized?(upload, extension, authorized_images(upload))
    86	  end
    87	
    88	  def maximum_image_file_size(upload)
    89	    maximum_file_size(upload, "image")
    90	  end
    91	
    92	  def authorized_attachment_extension(upload, extension)
    93	    extension_authorized?(upload, extension, authorized_attachments(upload))
    94	  end
    95	
    96	  def maximum_attachment_file_size(upload)
    97	    maximum_file_size(upload, "attachment")
    98	  end
    99	
   100	  private
   101	
   102	  def site_setting_type_hash(setting_name)
   103	    return {} if setting_name.blank?
   104	    SiteSetting.type_supervisor.type_hash(setting_name.to_sym)
   105	  end
   106	
   107	  def validate_site_setting_file_size(upload, setting_opts)
   108	    return if !upload.validate_file_size
   109	
   110	    max_size_kb = setting_opts[:max_file_size_kb]
   111	
   112	    if max_size_kb.present?
   113	      max_size_bytes = max_size_kb.to_i.kilobytes
   114	      if upload.filesize > max_size_bytes
   115	        upload.errors.add(
   116	          :filesize,
   117	          I18n.t(
   118	            "upload.attachments.too_large_humanized",
   119	            max_size: ActiveSupport::NumberHelper.number_to_human_size(max_size_bytes),
   120	          ),
   121	        )
   122	      end
   123	    elsif FileHelper.is_supported_image?(upload.original_filename)
   124	      maximum_image_file_size(upload)
   125	    else
   126	      maximum_attachment_file_size(upload)
   127	    end
   128	  end
   129	
   130	  def extension_allowed_for_site_setting?(extension, authorized_extensions)
   131	    extensions_to_set(authorized_extensions).include?(extension.downcase)
   132	  end
   133	
   134	  def extensions_to_set(exts)
   135	    extensions = Set.new
   136	
   137	    exts
   138	      .gsub(/[\s\.]+/, "")
   139	      .downcase
   140	      .split("|")
   141	      .each { |extension| extensions << extension if extension.exclude?("*") }
   142	
   143	    extensions
   144	  end
   145	
   146	  def authorized_extensions(upload)
   147	    extensions =
   148	      if upload.for_theme
   149	        SiteSetting.theme_authorized_extensions
   150	      elsif upload.for_export
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true

     3	class UploadSettingValidator
     4	  def initialize(opts = {})
     5	    @opts = opts
     6	  end
     7	
     8	  def valid_value?(val)
     9	    return true if val.blank?
    10	
    11	    upload = Upload.find_by(id: val)
    12	    upload.present? && additional_validation_passed(upload)
    13	  end
    14	
    15	  def error_message
    16	    return I18n.t("site_settings.errors.invalid_svg") if @opts[:name] == :splash_screen_image
    17	    I18n.t("site_settings.errors.invalid_upload")
    18	  end
    19	
    20	  def additional_validation_passed(upload)
    21	    if @opts[:name] == :splash_screen_image
    22	      validate_svg(upload)
    23	    else
    24	      true
    25	    end

     6	  class UploadsRestorer

     9	    S3_ENDPOINT_REGEX = /\.s3(?:\.dualstack\.[a-z0-9\-]+?|[.\-][a-z0-9\-]+?)?\.amazonaws\.com/
    10	
    11	    def self.s3_regex_string(s3_base_url)
    12	      clean_url = s3_base_url.sub(S3_ENDPOINT_REGEX, ".s3.amazonaws.com")
    13	
    14	      regex_string =
    15	        clean_url
    16	          .split(".s3.amazonaws.com")
    17	          .map { |s| Regexp.escape(s) }
    18	          .insert(1, S3_ENDPOINT_REGEX.source)
    19	          .join("")
    20	
    21	      [regex_string, clean_url]
    22	    end

    24	    def initialize(logger)

    28	    def restore(tmp_directory)
    29	      upload_directories =
